Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n//!\\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n//!\\n//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n//!\\n//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n//!\\n//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n//!\\n//! # Request flow\\n//!\\n//! ```text\\n//! TCP accept\\n//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n//!             └─ route handler\\n//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n//!                  └─ handler logic\\n//! ```\\n//!\\n//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\npub mod actor_scope;\\npub mod auth;\\nmod collection_listing;\\npub(crate) mod axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst ISSUER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"test-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"user-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"super-secret-key-for-tests\\\";\\nconst TEST_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_claims() -\u003e JwtClaims {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: Grants {\\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\\n            }],\\n        },\\n    }\\n}\\n\\nfn make_storage() -\u003e MemoryStorageAdapter {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Test User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    storage\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let storage: Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e =\\n        Box::new(make_storage());\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service call should succeed\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .expect(\\\"body read should succeed\\\");\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn get_req(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Request\u003cBody\u003e {\\n    Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(path)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer_req(path: \u0026str, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Request\u003cBody\u003e {\\n    Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(path)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\"))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn error_code(json: \u0026Value) -\u003e \u0026str {\\n    json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\")\\n}\\n\\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[test]\\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let claims = valid_claims();\\n    let token = issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\");\\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(\u0026token).expect(\\\"verify should succeed\\\");\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\\n}\\n\\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(error_code(\u0026json), \\\"unauthenticated\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\\n assert_eq!(error_code(\u0026json), \\\"credential_malformed\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\\n     databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n            }],\\n        },\\n    };\\n    let token = Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\n//!\\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\\n\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::body::Body;\\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\\nuse axum::routing::get;\\nuse axum::Router;\\nuse http::Request;\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{\\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\\n};\\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\nconst ISSUER: \u0026str = \\\"integration-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"u-integration-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"integration-test-secret\\\";\\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Integration User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\\n            Box::new(storage) as Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\\n        )),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service should not fail\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .unwrap_or_default();\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer(token: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_token(issuer: \u0026JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -\u003e String {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants,\\n    };\\n    issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn orders_read_grants() -\u003e Grants {\\n    Grants {\\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n        }],\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"unauthenticated\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test token = valid_token(\u0026state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", bearer(\u0026token))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\\n    let state = URL says \\\"acme\\\"\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\\n    };\\n    let token = .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", bearer(\u0026token))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"credential_wrong_tenant\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. data.text: The work-related subset is green, so the remaining leak is outside the files I touched. I’m looking at the few cmd tests that install the shared Fizeau service stub to see if any of them forget to reset it, because that’s the kind of global state that can poison later policy-selection tests.

			SessionID:           "sess-hint",

	root := factory.NewRootCommand()

	out, err := executeCommand(root, "try", "hint-bead-001", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
	require.NoError(t, err, "ddx try with a hard bead hint must succeed: %s", out)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "routing intent: difficulty=hard powerClass=smart source=bead_hint")

	events, err := store.Events("hint-bead-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var intentEvent *bead.BeadEvent
	for i := range events {
		if events[i].Kind == "execution-routing-intent" {
			intentEvent = &events[i]
			break
		}
	}
	require.NotNil(t, intentEvent, "expected execution-routing-intent evidence on the bead")

	var body map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(intentEvent.Body), &body))
	assert.Equal(t, "bead_hint", body["routing_intent_source"])
	assert.Equal(t, "hard", body["estimated_difficulty"])
	assert.Equal(t, "smart", body["requested_power_class"])
	assert.Equal(t, "claude", body["actual_harness"])
	assert.Equal(t, "anthropic", body["actual_provider"])
	assert.Equal(t, "claude-sonnet-4-6", body["actual_model"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(91), body["actual_power"])
}

func TestTryZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauPolicyWithoutInitialMinPower(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
	stub.listPolicies, stub.listModels = canonicalFizeauPolicyFixture()

	dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "README.md"), []byte("# test\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "init", dir).Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.email", "test@example.com").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.name", "Test User").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "add", ".").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "commit", "-m", "init").Run())
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), &bead.Bead{
		ID:        "ddx-zero-config-try-powerClass-standard",
		Title:     "Try with inferred standard routing powerClass",
		IssueType: "bug",
	}))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(dir)
	factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(
		root,
		"try",
		"ddx-zero-config-try-powerClass-standard",
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	)

	stub.mu.Lock()
	executeCalled := stub.executeCalled
	lastReq := stub.lastReq
	stub.mu.Unlock()
	require.True(t, executeCalled, "ddx try must invoke the implementation dispatch; output=%q err=%v", out, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "default", lastReq.Policy, "dispatch should request the ordinary no-requirement Fizeau policy by metadata")
	assert.Equal(t, 0, lastReq.MinPower, "initial zero-config dispatch must not duplicate the selected policy floor as MinPower")
	assert.Empty(t, lastReq.Harness, "zero-config routing must not hard-pin a harness")
	assert.Empty(t, lastReq.Provider, "zero-config routing must not hard-pin a provider")
	assert.Empty(t, lastReq.Model, "zero-config routing must not hard-pin a model")
}

func TestTryZeroConfigProviderConnectivityRetryAddsExactMinPowerFloor(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
	stub.listPolicies, stub.listModels = canonicalFizeauPolicyFixture()
	implementerCalls := 0
	stub.executeFn = func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
		ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
		if req.Role != "implementer" {
			ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"ready\",\"rationale\":\"ok\",\"readiness_checks\":[],\"score\":9,\"suggested_fixes\":[],\"waivers_applied\":[],\"recommended_action\":\"release_claim_retry\",\"suggested_amendments\":[],\"suggested_followup_beads\":[]}"}`)}
			close(ch)
			return ch, nil
		}
		implementerCalls++
		if implementerCalls == 1 {
			ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"error","exit_code":1,"error":"provider request failed: dial tcp 100.70.199.113:1235: connect: connection refused","routing_actual":{"provider":"vidar","model":"qwen-local","power":5}}`)}
		} else {
			ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}
		}
		close(ch)
		return ch, nil
	}

	dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "README.md"), []byte("# test\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "init", dir).Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.email", "test@example.com").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.name", "Test User").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "add", ".").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "commit", "-m", "init").Run())
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), &bead.Bead{
		ID:        "ddx-zero-config-try-provider-connectivity-retry",
		Title:     "Try routes around failed provider connectivity",
		IssueType: "bug",
	}))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(dir)
	factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(
		root,
		"try",
		"ddx-zero-config-try-provider-connectivity-retry",
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	)

	requests := capturedImplementationRequests(stub)
	require.Len(t, requests, 2, "ddx try should use the shared try-loop retry state machine; output=%q err=%v", out, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "default", requests[0].Policy)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, requests[0].MinPower)
	assert.Equal(t, "default", requests[1].Policy)
	assert.Equal(t, 6, requests[1].MinPower)
}

data.text: I’ve ruled out the obvious stub-leak path in `run`/`try`/`work`. The remaining suspicion is a shared routing/profile cache in the command package, so I’m reading the policy-selection helper that feeds these zero-config work tests.

The policy-selection helper itself is per-command and doesn’t obviously leak. I’m now scanning the rest of `cmd` tests for raw `os.Setenv` or other mutable global writes, because that’s the next most likely source of an order-dependent failure in the full suite.

// TestAcceptance_US007_ConfigureDDxSettings tests US-007: Configure DDX Settings
func TestAcceptance_US007_ConfigureDDxSettings(t *testing.T) {

	t.Run("view_current_configuration", func(t *testing.T) {
		// AC: Given I want to view settings, when I run `ddx config`, then the current configuration is displayed in readable format

		// Setup temp project directory
		tempDir := t.TempDir()

		// Create a basic .ddx/config.yaml config file using DDx structure
		config := `version: "2.0"
library:
  path: "./library"
  repository:
    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
    branch: "main"
persona_bindings:
  author: "Test User"
  email: "test@example.com"
`
		ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ddxroot.DirName)
		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
		configPath := filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml")
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(configPath, []byte(config), 0644))

		factory := NewCommandFactory(tempDir)
		rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()
		output, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "config", "export")

		require.NoError(t, err, "Config export should work")
		assert.Contains(t, output, "Test User", "Should show author")
		assert.Contains(t, output, "test@example.com", "Should show email")
		assert.Contains(t, output, "https://github.com/test/repo", "Should show repository URL")

		// Output should be in readable YAML format
		assert.Contains(t, output, "version:", "Should show version key")
		assert.Contains(t, output, "author:", "Should show author key")
	})

	t.Run("set_configuration_value", func(t *testing.T) {
		// AC: Given I want to change a setting, when I run `ddx config set <key> <value>`, then the setting is updated and confirmed

		// Unset environment variable that would override config file
		originalLibPath := os.Getenv("DDX_LIBRARY_BASE_PATH")
		_ = os.Unsetenv("DDX_LIBRARY_BASE_PATH")
		defer func() {
			if originalLibPath != "" {
				_ = os.Setenv("DDX_LIBRARY_BASE_PATH", originalLibPath)
			}
		}()

		tempDir := t.TempDir()

		// Create initial config using DDx structure
		config := `version: "2.0"
persona_bindings:
  author: "Old User"
`
		ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ddxroot.DirName)
		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
		configPath := filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml")
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(configPath, []byte(config), 0644))

		factory := NewCommandFactory(tempDir)
		rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

		// Set a new value using library namespace
		output, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "config", "set", "library.path", "./new-library")
		require.NoError(t, err, "Config set should work")
		assert.Contains(t, output, "./new-library", "Should confirm the new value")
		assert.Contains(t, output, "library.path", "Should mention the key being set")

		// Verify the value was actually set
		getCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()
		getOutput, err := executeCommand(getCmd, "config", "get", "library.path")
		require.NoError(t, err, "Config get should work")
		assert.Contains(t, getOutput, "./new-library", "Should retrieve the updated value")
	})

	t.Run("get_specific_configuration_value", func(t *testing.T) {
		// AC: Given I need a specific value, when I run `ddx config get <key>`, then the current value for that key is displayed

		tempDir := t.TempDir()

library:

persona_bindings:
  author: "Specific User"
`

		factory := NewCommandFactory(tempDir)

		// Get repository URL using library namespace

		require.NoError(t, err, "Config get repository URL should work")
		assert.Contains(t, output, "https://github.com/specific/repo", "Should show repository URL")

I found a package-level profile snapshot cache. That’s a real candidate for cross-test bleed, so I’m checking how the cache key is built before deciding whether a test-only reset is needed or whether the failing work test is actually inheriting a stale snapshot.

data.text: I think I found the shape of the leak: the profile snapshot cache in `cli/internal/agent/profile_select.go` is keyed by service pointer, so stale snapshots can survive across tests if the allocator reuses an address. I’m checking whether there’s already a test reset hook before I add one, because that would explain the order-dependent policy mismatch cleanly.

package cmd

// run_cmd_test.go verifies ddx-0248f921 AC:
//   - ddx run --help exits 0 and describes the layer-1 command.
//   - ddx run forwards harness/provider/model/profile to Execute.

import (
	"os"

)

// TestRunCommandHelp verifies AC1: ddx run --help exits 0.
func TestRunCommandHelp(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "run", "--help")
	require.NoError(t, err, "ddx run --help must exit 0")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "run", "help output must describe the run command")
}

// TestRunCommandRegisteredInRoot verifies AC2: the root command exposes run.
func TestRunCommandRegisteredInRoot(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	root := NewCommandFactory(minimalProjectDir(t)).NewRootCommand()
	runCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"run"})
	require.NoError(t, err, "root command must register ddx run")
	require.NotNil(t, runCmd, "root command must expose ddx run")
	assert.Equal(t, "run", runCmd.Use)
}

// TestRunPassthroughHarnessModelProviderToExecute verifies AC3: ddx run
// forwards --harness, --model, and --provider to ServiceExecuteRequest without
// calling ResolveRoute (CONTRACT-003).
func TestRunPassthroughHarnessModelProviderToExecute(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)

	dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
	_, err := executeCommand(root, "run",
		"--harness", "codex",
		"--model", "gpt-5.4",
		"--provider", "openai",
		"--text", "hello",
		"--timeout", "5s",
	)
	require.NoError(t, err, "ddx run must succeed; ResolveRoute call would fail the stub")

	stub.mu.Lock()
	executeCalled := stub.executeCalled
	lastReq := stub.lastReq
	stub.mu.Unlock()

	require.True(t, executeCalled, "Execute must be called")
	assert.Equal(t, "codex", lastReq.Harness, "Harness must pass through unchanged")
	assert.Equal(t, "gpt-5.4", lastReq.Model, "Model must pass through unchanged")
	assert.Equal(t, "openai", lastReq.Provider, "Provider must pass through unchanged")
}

// TestRunPassthroughProfileToExecute verifies AC3: ddx run forwards
// --profile to ServiceExecuteRequest.Policy.
func TestRunPassthroughProfileToExecute(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)

	dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
	_, err := executeCommand(root, "run",
		"--profile", "smart",
		"--text", "hello",
		"--timeout", "5s",
	)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	stub.mu.Lock()
	executeCalled := stub.executeCalled
	lastReq := stub.lastReq
	stub.mu.Unlock()

	require.True(t, executeCalled, "Execute must be called")
	assert.Equal(t, "smart", lastReq.Policy, "Policy must pass through unchanged")
}

func TestRunPassthroughMinMaxPowerToExecute(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

data.text:
Dirty-root suppression is now wired into `ddx work focus`, and the full cmd test suite stayed green after a small test-helper cache fix.

- [`cli/cmd/work_focus.go`](/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d241d9e4-20260627T205852-aa732710/cli/cmd/work_focus.go#L149) now checks canonical dirty tracked paths, surfaces them in `unknowns`, and replaces the worker-start recommendation with a resolve/commit/stash message when the project root is dirty.
- [`cli/cmd/work_focus_test.go`](/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d241d9e4-20260627T205852-aa732710/cli/cmd/work_focus_test.go#L198) adds `TestWorkFocusDirtyProjectRootSuppressesWorkerRecommendation` and keeps the clean deep-queue recommendation coverage.
- [`cli/cmd/run_test_helpers_test.go`](/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d241d9e4-20260627T205852-aa732710/cli/cmd/run_test_helpers_test.go#L21) and [`cli/cmd/run_cmd_test.go`](/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d241d9e4-20260627T205852-aa732710/cli/cmd/run_cmd_test.go#L172) now return non-comparable test-service values so profile snapshot caching can’t bleed across cmd tests.

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestWorkFocus(DirtyProjectRootSuppressesWorkerRecommendation|RecommendsWorkerForDeepReadyQueue)'`
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestDirtyRootPreventsClaimLoop`
- `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./cmd`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

Commit: `0f1185950` - `fix: suppress dirty-root worker recommendation [ddx-d241d9e4]`
